summary: Set 030 Session 2: Phase 2 dual-write writers shipped per spec D5. ai_router/session_state.py: SCHEMA_VERSION 2->3; register_session_start and _flip_state_to_closed emit BOTH v3 sessions[] and the legacy progress triple (currentSession / totalSessions / completedSessions derived from sessions[] via _derive_legacy_fields). Five new helpers (_existing_sessions_records, _spec_titles_for_set, _build_sessions_array, _derive_legacy_fields, _validate_sessions_or_raise). Scaffolding (_not_started_payload, _backfill_payload) writes v3 when totalSessions is known. Writer-side invariant enforcement is fail-loud per spec D6 - SessionStateInvariantError raised BEFORE any file write or event emission. New forced-incident-recovery semantic: force=True promotes every session in sessions[] to complete so rule 7 holds by construction. Round A verification (gpt-5-4, $0.266495) found 4 must-fix issues: (1) silent out-of-range truncation in _build_sessions_array; (2) natural last-session close promoting all; (3) unvalidated legacy-only fallback; (4) work_started event emitted before validation. All four addressed with +7 regression tests. Round B verification (gpt-5-4, $0.193638) VERIFIED line-by-line. Tests: 529 pytest passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected (+45 from new test_session_state_v3.py + Round-A regression tests + v2 file updates for v3 dual-write shape). TypeScript tsc --noEmit clean. Operator-visible behavior change: currentSession is now derived strictly as the in-progress session's number (or null when no session is in-flight), resolving the v2 'in-flight OR most-recently-closed' ambiguity called out in the spec problem statement. Consumer-repo reader migration ships in Session 3.

reason.specifics: Session 3 migrates readers (close-out gates, the extension tree provider, the reconciler, repair logic) from direct legacy-field reads to get_progress() calls and adds the D13 lint rule. Cross-cutting refactor across Python and TypeScript; the writer-side contract Session 2 just shipped is the load-bearing precondition. Keeping Claude Opus 4.7 @ effort=high preserves the architectural context of what dual-write parity guarantees the readers can rely on and how the Explorer's 'Done' to 'Complete' label migration interacts with the v3 status glossary.
